topic: ObservationConsolidator — Two-Tier Memory Aggregation
summary:
## Purpose
Aggregates raw observations into two tiers — daily Digests and weekly persistent Insights — via LLM-driven synthesis. Provides content displayed in the VKB dashboard's Digests and Insights pages. Includes built-in overlap detection, structured merge/facet classification, automated code-verification of insight accuracy, and auto-verification cadence enforcement.

## Architecture
- **Digests** — daily thematic consolidation of raw observations; time-triggered, cross-agent
- **Insights** — weekly persistent knowledge synthesis built from Digests; current corpus: **61 insights**, 5 facet-linked
- **VKB content source**: Pipeline 1 — ETM → SQLite via obs_api → `ObservationConsolidator.js` → VKB dashboard; not the wave-analysis `insight-generator.ts` pipeline
- **Insight format**: `_buildInsightPrompt()` enforces structured reference-article format (Purpose, Architecture, Key Files, Usage, Troubleshooting); not changelog/digest-style entries
- **Overlap detection**: `_findSimilarInsightId()` returns a structured verdict object `{ verdict: 'merge' | 'facet' | 'none', ... }` with `TOPIC_STOPWORDS` filtering to suppress stopword-driven false matches; uses topic-only token comparison (full-text Jaccard produced false negatives); `synthesizeInsights` auto-merges on `merge` verdict and writes cross-link edges on `facet` verdict
- **Corpus audit**: `scripts/audit-knowledge-overlap.mjs` performs topic-only batch comparison to detect drift; previously identified 19/70 insights in 8 topic clusters; run on-demand when corpus accumulation is suspected
- **Code-verification**: extraction and verification methods upstream of prompt-building; each insight carries a `verificationRatio` (current average: **0.9**); auto-verification runs by default with a **7-day cadence guard** (will not re-verify more than once per 7 days per insight); path-resolution bug in the verifier is fixed
- **Known gap**: retrieval ranking and confidence decay do not yet weight by `verificationRatio` score; freshness reranking (recency of last verification) is handled separately by `retrieval-service.js`
- **Chunk-level LLM failure**: failed LLM calls log `"LLM call failed for ... skipping chunk"` and silently skip — no retry, no partial output
- **KG entity integration**: auto-creates project anchor entities and POSTs relation edges via `api-routes.js`
- **Progress events**: per-stage events emitted; heartbeat records `lastStderrAt` for reliable staleness detection
- **Process lifecycle**: `consolidator.close()` must explicitly close all Redis and DB connections or the Node.js event loop hangs indefinitely

## Key Files
- `src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — consolidation logic; `_buildInsightPrompt()`, `_findSimilarInsightId()` verdict system, `TOPIC_STOPWORDS`, code-verification methods, auto-verification with 7-day cadence guard, `synthesizeInsights`, progress events, `close()`
- `src/live-logging/ObservationExporter.js` — shared export module; tombstone resurrection prevention (deleted/tombstoned IDs are excluded from export)
- `src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js` — intended future trigger point for auto-consolidation
- `scripts/audit-knowledge-overlap.mjs` — topic-only overlap audit script; run on-demand when corpus drift is suspected
- `scripts/consolidate-insight-clusters.mjs` — one-time migration script (already run; reduced corpus from 70 → 62; subsequent cleanup reduced to 61)
- `.data/insight-consolidation-plan.md` — cluster classification reference for the consolidation run
- `scripts/backfill-raw-observations.js` — recovery tool for undigested raw observations filtered by `confidence=low`
- `api-routes.js` — relation endpoints; links insight entities to project anchors

## Usage
Dashboard exposes tiers via `/api/digests` and `/api/insights`. Manually invoke consolidation for missing date ranges. To change insight format, update `_buildInsightPrompt()`, delete old insights from SQLite, and trigger re-synthesis. Run `audit-knowledge-overlap.mjs` periodically to detect topic cluster accumulation. Auto-verification fires on a 7-day guard; force a re-verify pass by clearing the cadence timestamp if a manual audit pass is needed sooner.

## Troubleshooting
- **`"LLM call failed for ... skipping chunk"` in logs**: LLM proxy issue (timeout, provider failure, misconfiguration) — check proxy health on `localhost:12435`, verify provider availability; affected chunks are silently skipped with no retry
- **VKB shows old-format insights after updating `insight-generator.ts`**: Wrong pipeline — update `_buildInsightPrompt()` in `ObservationConsolidator.js` instead
- **Duplicate insights appear after re-synthesis**: `_findSimilarInsightId()` returning `none` incorrectly — verify `TOPIC_STOPWORDS` is not over-stripping meaningful tokens; check that topic-only comparison is active (not full-text Jaccard)
- **Facet cross-links missing after merge run**: `synthesizeInsights` must check for `facet` verdict and emit cross-link edges — verify verdict shape is being read correctly
- **Low `verificationRatio` on specific insights**: Run code-verification pass; check path-resolution in the verifier; deleted or relocated code paths lower the ratio
- **Auto-verification not running**: 7-day cadence guard may be blocking — inspect cadence timestamps per insight; clear the timestamp to force immediate re-verification
- **No Digests for recent dates**: Daemon is not auto-started — manually invoke; also check `confidence=low` filter and run `backfill-raw-observations.js` to recover
- **Consolidator process hangs after completing**: Unclosed Redis or DB connection in `consolidator.close()` — verify all connections are explicitly closed
- **Deleted observations reappearing in export output**: Tombstone resurrection bug — verify `ObservationExporter.js` tombstone prevention patch is deployed; deleted IDs must be tracked and excluded at export time

metadata.scope: src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js and the consolidation pipeline that produces Digests and Insights from raw observations

topic: LLM Token Usage Tracking — Proxy Instrumentation and Dashboard
summary:
## Purpose
`rapid-llm-proxy`'s token usage module records per-process LLM token consumption in hourly bucketed JSON files, keyed by user hash and time window, so the system health dashboard can visualise token spend without requiring centralised aggregation.

## Architecture
- **`src/token-usage.ts`** — core tracking module. Key functions:
  - `resolveTokenExportDir()` — determines the export directory path; overridden by `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` env var
  - `currentWindow()` — computes the current hourly time window string using `LSL_TIMEZONE`
  - `lslWritePath()` — constructs the full path for the current hour's JSON file, incorporating `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` in the filename
  - `exportToHourFile()` — writes/appends token usage data to the hourly JSON file; SELECT and INSERT OR IGNORE statements both include `model_raw`; files missing `model_raw` fall back to the `model` field for backward compatibility
  - Per-window-keyed debounce `Map` — throttles writes so that high-frequency completions within the same hour do not produce excessive I/O; each window key tracks its own debounce state independently
- **Startup wrapper** (`bin/start-llm-proxy.sh`) — sets `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` to `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/` (under the private coding-history repo), co-locating token exports with LSL transcripts; also exports:
  - `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` — deterministic short hash (e.g., `c197ef`) computed via env-var-driven dynamic import of `src/user-hash-generator.js`; the dynamic-import pattern (not `import(process.argv[1])`) prevents stdout contamination
  - `LSL_TIMEZONE` — user's local timezone (e.g., `Europe/Berlin`); inserted between the final `fi` block and the `exec node` log line in `start-llm-proxy.sh`
- **Export layout**: per-hour JSON files in `year-month/` subdirectories under the `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` path (default: `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/`); filename includes `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` for user-scoped attribution; format mirrors LSL conventions
- **Schema**: export rows include `model_raw` (raw provider model string) alongside canonicalized `model`; `model_raw` survives full export → hydrate round-trips without data loss
- **Dashboard visualisation**: `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/token-usage.tsx` — Recharts-based view including treemap; treemap currently has no `Tooltip` component (smaller boxes non-interactive)
- **PlantUML diagrams** (`docs/diagrams/`): three in-place diagrams (kebab-case, no versioned copies) — `component-model.puml`, `request-flow.puml`, `architecture-overview.puml`

## Key Files
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/token-usage.ts` — hourly bucketing; `resolveTokenExportDir`, `currentWindow`, `lslWritePath`, `exportToHourFile`, per-window debounce `Map`; `model_raw` in SELECT and INSERT OR IGNORE
- `rapid-llm-proxy/bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` — sets `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` to private history repo path; exports `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` and `LSL_TIMEZONE` before bridge spawn
- `coding/scripts/user-hash-generator.js` — hash computation in the coding repo; imported dynamically by `start-llm-proxy.sh` via `node -e "import(process.env.USER_HASH_SCRIPT)..."` to avoid stdout pollution from `runIfMain` blocks
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — main bridge; routes completions, records token counts
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/token-usage.tsx` — dashboard visualisation; treemap missing `Tooltip`
- `rapid-llm-proxy/docs/diagrams/` — architecture PlantUML sources; updated in-place, PNGs regenerated alongside

## Usage
`token-usage.ts` runs inside the bridge process. `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH`, `LSL_TIMEZONE`, and `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` must be present in the bridge environment for correct per-user, per-timezone hourly file naming and correct output location — all three are injected by `start-llm-proxy.sh`. Export files accumulate under `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/<year-month>/` in the private coding-history repo. The dashboard reads them at render time. Restart via `launchctl` after changes to `start-llm-proxy.sh` or `token-usage.ts` (TypeScript must be rebuilt first).

## Troubleshooting
- **`LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` is a multi-line garbage string**: `start-llm-proxy.sh` was using `import(process.argv[1])`, which triggered `runIfMain()` and contaminated stdout — verify the env-var-driven `user-hash-generator.js` import pattern is in place; revert via `git checkout` if corrupted and restart launchd
- **`LSL_TIMEZONE` missing from bridge environment**: Check that the export line in `start-llm-proxy.sh` is placed between the final `fi` and the `exec node` log line; verify `launchctl` restarted after the change
- **Token export files writing to wrong location**: `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` not set or overridden — verify `start-llm-proxy.sh` exports it pointing to `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/`; confirm `launchctl` restarted after the change
- **`model_raw` missing from exported rows**: Verify `token-usage.ts` SELECT statement includes `model_raw` and INSERT OR IGNORE includes it; legacy files without `model_raw` should fall back to `model` — ensure fallback is present in hydration logic
- **Hourly export files not created / empty**: Confirm `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` and `LSL_TIMEZONE` are non-empty in the bridge process; verify `resolveTokenExportDir()` resolves to a writable path; check that `exportToHourFile()` is being called after each completion
- **Per-window debounce suppressing expected writes**: Each hourly window key maintains an independent debounce timer; if a window rolls over mid-session, the first write of the new window fires immediately — verify window key computation in `currentWindow()` matches `LSL_TIMEZONE`
- **Model names show inconsistent spellings in token usage view**: Raw strings from uncanonicalized call sites in `server.mjs` — implement `canonicalizeModelName()` at each log call site
- **PlantUML diagrams out of date after architecture changes**: Diagrams live in `docs/diagrams/`; update in-place (no versioned copies) and regenerate PNGs alongside source changes

metadata.scope: Token usage recording, export pipeline, and dashboard visualisation in `rapid-llm-proxy`

topic: LLM CLI Proxy — VPN/Corporate Network Detection
summary:
## Purpose
Provides a local HTTP proxy bridge that routes LLM completion requests to one of five providers (claude-code, Copilot, Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic) based on network context and optional process-level override pins. Distinct from the TypeScript `integrations/llm-cli-proxy` service.

## Architecture
**Provider priority order**:
- **Home/public network**: `claude-code → copilot → groq → openai → anthropic`
- **Corporate/VPN network**: `copilot → claude-code → groq → openai → anthropic`

**Network mode detection**: `bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` <USER_ID_REDACTED> the health coordinator's `/health/state` endpoint for network mode (e.g., `[N:HOME] [P:OFF]`) at startup — eliminates split-brain where startup and statusline reported different modes. The coordinator is the single source of truth.

**User hash and timezone injection** (Phase 36): `start-llm-proxy.sh` now computes and exports two additional env vars before spawning the bridge:
- `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` — computed via env-var-driven dynamic import of `user-hash-generator.js`; the dynamic-import approach (not `import(process.argv[1])`) avoids stdout contamination that caused multi-line garbage values in earlier attempts; confirmed `c197ef`
- `LSL_TIMEZONE` — exported from the startup script, placed between the final `fi` block and the `exec node` log line; confirmed `Europe/Berlin`

**Provider registration**: env-gated `init` blocks in `server.mjs` register `openai` and `groq` when their API key env vars are present; loaded by `start-llm-proxy.sh` at startup.

**Dispatcher**: `switch` statement in `server.mjs` routes to `completeOpenAI` and `completeGroq` functions.

**Process-level hard pin** (Stage-0 override): when active, unconditionally replaces `body.provider` and `body.model` even when the caller explicitly sends a provider; falls through only if the pinned provider is unreachable.

**CORS**: `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` must include `PUT`; a missing `PUT` entry blocks all preflight requests while `GET` succeeds silently.

**Settings routes**: `server.mjs` exposes `GET /settings` and `PUT /settings` for user-facing model configuration.

## Key Files
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — routing, dispatcher switch, CORS headers (must include `PUT`), Stage-0 override logic, `GET`/`PUT` settings routes
- `rapid-llm-proxy/bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` — startup; <USER_ID_REDACTED> `/health/state`; loads API key env vars; computes `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` via `user-hash-generator.js` dynamic import; exports `LSL_TIMEZONE`
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/user-hash-generator.js` — user hash computation module; imported dynamically in `start-llm-proxy.sh`
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/token-usage.ts` — hourly bucketing for token usage reporting

## Usage
Runs as a background launchd service. Network mode detected once at startup by querying the health coordinator. `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` and `LSL_TIMEZONE` are available to the bridge process for token-usage file naming and timezone-aware time windows. Restart via `launchctl` after changes.

## Troubleshooting
- **`LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` contains multi-line garbage**: Startup script was using `import(process.argv[1])` which triggered `runIfMain()` and polluted stdout — verify `start-llm-proxy.sh` uses the env-var-driven `user-hash-generator.js` dynamic import pattern; revert via `git checkout` if corrupted and restart launchd
- **All requests routed to Copilot regardless of provider**: Stage-0 override pin is being bypassed — verify unconditional override logic in `server.mjs`
- **`PUT /settings` fails with CORS preflight error**: `PUT` missing from `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` in `server.mjs`
- **Statusline shows home but startup detected corporate (split-brain)**: Startup script is not querying `/health/state` — verify `start-llm-proxy.sh` does not run its own `curl --proxy` probe
- **Groq/OpenAI calls silently routed to Copilot despite keys present**: Dispatcher `switch` missing branches or env-gated init blocks not executed — verify both branches and API key env vars
- **Bridge starts with wrong network mode**: Health coordinator not running at startup — ensure coordinator is up on port 3034 before bridge launches

metadata.scope: The `rapid-llm-proxy` proxy bridge (`proxy-bridge/server.mjs`) and its startup wrapper (`bin/start-llm-proxy.sh`), including network mode detection, provider routing, and per-process environment variable injection.

topic: Health Coordinator — Phase 33 Consolidated Health Monitoring
summary:
## Purpose
Consolidates all service probing into a single coordinator process that exposes health results and system-wide state over HTTP, eliminating split-brain between consumers and removing bind-mount mismatches between host and container `.health/` directories.

## Architecture
- **Coordinator daemon**: `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — Express server on port **3034**; LaunchAgent plist; probes services on 30s cycle; maps results to sub-check keys consumed by frontend
- **Registry file**: tracks coordinator PID and `startTime`; stale entries (PID no longer alive) cause dashboard to report "degraded" status — this is correct behavior, not a bug. The uptime display uses `startTime`-to-now (not last heartbeat), so stale entries show misleadingly large uptime values (e.g., 341h for a 13-day-old entry). Fix: clear the stale registry entry manually, or update uptime calculation to use last heartbeat timestamp instead of `startTime`.
- **Semantic LLM probe**: sends `"say OK"` prompt and validates response with `/ok/i` regex — minimises input and output token overhead to a single token per probe, ~1s round-trip; `semantic_ok=true` on match
- **Network/proxy state**: polls `location`, `proxy_running`, `proxy_functional`, `internet_reachable` on 30s interval; exposes via `/health/state`; this is the **single source of truth** for all consumers (statusline, dashboard, proxy wrapper) — consumers must not detect network mode independently
- **LSL time window**: `/health/state` exposes `lsl_meta.current_window` computed by `getTimeWindow()` / `utcToLocalTime()` from `timezone-utils.js`, with `'unknown'` fallback; `timezone-utils.js` is the canonical time-window source so the proxy, statusline, and dashboard all share one reference without recomputing it
- **`state.network` forwarding**: `handleGetHealthStatus` in `server.js` must explicitly pass the `network` object to the frontend; omitting it causes `healthStatus.network` to always be `undefined`
- **obs_api auto-heal FSM**: triggers restart on "unreachable" status; `restartObsApi` in `health-remediation-actions.js`; `AUTO_HEAL_MAP` includes obs_api
- **`'corporate'` network mode**: coordinator must accept `'corporate'` as a valid proxy network mode; unrecognised modes fall through to `'unknown'` and trigger false violations
- **ETM safety-net auto-spawn**: coordinator detects ETM not running and auto-respawns; `auto_heal`/`auto_heal_action` fields must be included in violation synthesis in `server.js`
- **LevelDB/Qdrant sub-check mapping**: probe results stored in `levelDB.available`/`qdrant.available` must map to legacy sub-check keys (`leveldb_lock_check`, `qdrant_availability`) that the frontend reads; restart coordinator to force re-probe
- **`toUiStatus` mapping**: must handle `'passed'` as a recognised status value
- **Rules engine**: reads `config/health-verification-rules.json` directly; `bind_mount_freshness` rule category deleted in Phase 33
- **SPA/API route order**: in `static-server.js`, `/api/*` reverse-proxy mounted **before** `express.static` and SPA catch-all
- **Variable collision**: Node.js `net` module conflicts with local network state — state references use `netState` throughout
- **Test script**: `scripts/test-coding.sh` probes the canonical `/health/state` endpoint; references to any deleted registry endpoint have been removed

## Key Files
- `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — coordinator daemon; semantic probe (`"say OK"` / `/ok/i`); `lsl_meta.current_window` via `getTimeWindow()`; `toUiStatus` with `'passed'`; `'corporate'` mode in valid-mode checks; registry file with PID and `startTime`
- `scripts/timezone-utils.js` — `getTimeWindow()` / `utcToLocalTime()`; canonical LSL time window source
- `config/health-verification-rules.json` — rules; hostnames must be `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`; `bind_mount_freshness` category deleted
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/static-server.js` — SPA server; `/api/*` proxy mounted before static/catch-all
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — reverse-proxies to coordinator; forwards `network` object; includes `auto_heal`/`auto_heal_action` in violation synthesis
- `scripts/health-remediation-actions.js` — includes `restartObsApi`
- `scripts/test-coding.sh` — integration test script; uses `/health/state` endpoint for all coordinator checks
- LaunchAgent plist — loads coordinator on host; `bootout`/`bootstrap` cycle required after env var changes

## Usage
Runs as a LaunchAgent on the host. All health consumers call it via HTTP on port 3034. `HEALTH_COORDINATOR_URL` env var controls where consumers send requests. Network/proxy state and LSL time window are authoritative outputs of this service; no consumer should compute either independently.

## Troubleshooting
- **Dashboard shows "degraded" coordinator status**: The coordinator registry file contains a stale PID entry for a process that no longer exists — clear the stale entry from the registry file; the 341h+ uptime display for such entries is expected (uses `startTime`-to-now, not last heartbeat)
- **Misleadingly large uptime display**: Uptime is calculated from `startTime`, not last heartbeat — for stale entries this produces inflated values; consider updating uptime calculation to use last heartbeat timestamp
- **Sub-checks show "unknown"**: Coordinator has not completed its first 30s probe cycle — restart to force re-probe; verify `toUiStatus` handles `'passed'`
- **`lsl_meta.current_window` is `'unknown'`**: `getTimeWindow()` failed or `timezone-utils.js` path is wrong — check coordinator logs; verify `utcToLocalTime()` has a valid timezone env var
- **Semantic probe shows high token cost**: Verify `scripts/health-coordinator.js` sends `"say OK"` (not longer prompts) and uses `/ok/i` for validation
- **`network` field always `undefined` on frontend**: `handleGetHealthStatus` in `server.js` not passing the `network` object — add it explicitly; check Redux reducer is not dropping it
- **Auto-heal badge shows "Manual" for obs_api**: `auto_heal`/`auto_heal_action` fields missing from violation synthesis in `server.js`
- **Network mode `'corporate'` triggers violation**: Add `'corporate'` to all mode comparisons in `health-coordinator.js`
- **Stale env vars after plist change**: Run `launchctl bootout` then `launchctl bootstrap` to restart with new environment
- **`bind_mount_freshness` rule causing recreate loop**: Remove stale rule entry; category is deleted in Phase 33
- **Test script fails on coordinator checks**: Verify `scripts/test-coding.sh` targets `/health/state`, not a deleted registry endpoint

metadata.scope: Coordinator daemon, service probing, health state API, and registry management

topic: Observation and Digest Retention — SQLite Pruning and JSON Cold-Store
summary:
## Purpose
Caps the SQLite `observations` table to a 7-day retention window while preserving older rows in a JSON cold-store, with the observations API transparently merging both sources for any query range so the dashboard shows a continuous history regardless of pruning.

## Architecture
Five components delivered across Phase 35 waves:

- **ObservationWriter retention floor** (35-01): enforces a minimum retention boundary on writes, preventing pruning of freshly-ingested observations; tested in `ObservationWriter.retention-floor.test.js`
- **ObservationPruner** (35-02): 91-line nightly job removes rows from `observations` beyond the 7-day boundary; `insights` table is permanently excluded from all DELETE statements; 5-test Jest suite
- **ColdStoreReader** (35-03): reads JSON cold-store files and maps them to the same row shape as SQLite results; 7-test Jest suite
- **Obs-API wiring** (35-04): pure helpers extracted into `scripts/observations-api-merge.mjs` (avoids a Jest/TS-dist chain breakage); back-compat re-exports maintained in `observations-api-server.mjs`; Set-based dedup logic carries load-bearing inline comments at both call sites; handler gate removed from `observations-api-server.mjs`
- **Full-union pagination** (35-07): fetches the entire SQLite in-range slice without LIMIT/OFFSET, unions with cold-store rows in-memory, deduplicates, sorts by `timestamp DESC`, then slices per page — replaces the former "offset=0-only-cold" contract where SQLite rows saturated all page slots and hid cold rows entirely

**Pagination total fix** (35-06): `_mergeObservations` previously reported `total = sqliteTotal + coldTotal`, overcounting; now reports the true merged-and-deduplicated count.

**Live data volumes**: ~1,855 total rows (712 SQLite + 1,143 cold) for the April 1–May 15 range; cold rows first appear on pages 15+ and are marked with a ❄ snowflake icon in the UI; `coldOnFirstPageOnly=false` confirmed via live probe.

**Known boot race**: if the pruner executes while the consolidator is mid-write during `SIGTERM`, the pruner may report `"database disk image is malformed"` — `PRAGMA integrity_check` confirms the DB is actually healthy; fix is a second obs-api restart after the WAL checkpoints.

**Known anomaly**: `_origin: null` appears on SQLite rows at `offset=200`; cold-store rows correctly carry `_origin: 'cold'` — flagged for follow-up.

## Key Files
- `src/live-logging/ObservationPruner.js` — nightly pruning; excludes `insights` table
- `src/live-logging/ColdStoreReader.js` — JSON cold-store reader; maps to SQLite row shape
- `scripts/observations-api-merge.mjs` — merge helper; full-union pagination; Set-based dedup
- `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` — API server; handler gate removed; delegates to merge helper
- `tests/scripts/observations-api-server.merge.test.js` — 38-test suite for merge logic
- `.planning/phases/35-observation-digest-retention-with-json-cold-store-fallback/` — per-wave PLAN and SUMMARY files

## Usage
Pruner runs nightly. To surface cold-store rows in the dashboard UI, set the date picker to a range older than 7 days — cold rows appear interleaved with SQLite rows, distinguished by the ❄ icon. The merge API is transparent to all consumers of `/api/observations`. Verify Phase 35 gate with: `curl 'http://localhost:12436/api/observations?limit=10'` and check for `fromColdStore: true`.

## Troubleshooting
- **Dashboard shows "No observations yet" on last pages**: Pagination total overcounting — verify `_mergeObservations` reports the true merged count, not `sqliteTotal + coldTotal`
- **Cold rows not visible despite date range spanning retention boundary**: Full-union pagination must be active — verify `observations-api-server.mjs` does not apply LIMIT/OFFSET to SQLite before the cold-store union; confirm handler gate removal is deployed
- **`"database disk image is malformed"` at pruner boot**: Boot race between consolidator SIGTERM and pruner execution — run `PRAGMA integrity_check` to confirm DB health, then restart obs-api to allow WAL checkpoint before retrying
- **Cold rows always zero / `fromColdStore: false`**: `ColdStoreReader.js` not wired into the API handler — verify Phase 35-04 wiring is deployed
- **`_origin: null` on some SQLite rows**: Known anomaly at offset=200; cold-store rows carry `_origin: 'cold'`; SQLite rows have no `_origin` set — no action required yet

metadata.scope: Five-wave Phase 35 implementation: ObservationPruner, ColdStoreReader, obs-api merge helper, full-union pagination, and pagination total fix across `src/live-logging/` and `scripts/`

topic: GSM Health Warning and Statusline Rendering
summary:
## Purpose
Renders a real-time health summary in the tmux status bar for all active projects, showing emoji badges for coordinator state, knowledge pipeline, LSL health, and system warnings. Degraded state is surfaced immediately on the next refresh cycle.

## Architecture
- **Rendering chain**: tmux config → `status-line-fast.cjs` (fast path with cache borrow) → `combined-status-line.js` (full render)
- **Fast path**: `status-line-fast.cjs` borrows a sibling cache file filtered by `-w${paneWidth}.txt` suffix to avoid cross-width contamination; cache TTL is **30s**; safety timer is **10s** (exceeds CSL's internal 8s timeout so CSL can emit its own `⚠️ SYS:TIMEOUT` rather than being silently killed)
- **Cascade protection**: `getCoordinatorState` in `combined-status-line.js` is hardened against transient `curl` failures to `:3034/health/state` — a single failed probe must not degrade all coordinator-derived badges simultaneously
- **Cell-width residue buffer**: A `CELL_DRIFT_BUFFER` (+2 cells) is applied in `leftPadToStableCellWidth` to compensate for a 1-cell mismatch between `visibleCellWidth`'s counting of the ⚠️ emoji and xterm.js rendering, preventing ghost characters bleeding through
- **Emoji width fix**: The OKB/books segment uses 🟡 (not ⚠️) for warning state. ⚠️ with VS16 variation selector causes a cell-width miscalculation that makes clock text bleed past the segment boundary. All affected locations in both `combined-status-line.js` and `status-line-fast.cjs` use 🟡.
- **Heartbeat override**: Any project reporting a `'healthy'` lifecycle rollup state is always displayed with 🟢, overriding stale transcript-derived icon logic in `combined-status-line.js`.
- **Health warning** (~line 1682): `[🏥⚠️]` shown when `violationCount > 0` OR `!gcmHealthy`; source is `/tmp/.../global-status.json`
- **Idle-state badges**: `isUserActive()` helper in `combined-status-line.js` detects inactive sessions and renders `⚫` instead of `⚠️`/degraded for `📚` and `🧠` badges, semantically distinguishing idle from broken
- **Failure logging**: `csl-failures.jsonl` collects failure events; can be monitored in a loop to watch for regressions
- **Cache files**: Width-suffixed files only (e.g., `combined-status-line-cache-coding-w220.txt`)

## Key Files
- `scripts/combined-status-line.js` — full statusline renderer; health warning ~line 1682; `getCoordinatorState` cascade protection; `leftPadToStableCellWidth` residue buffer; `isUserActive()` idle detection; heartbeat 🟢 override
- `scripts/status-line-fast.cjs` — fast-path entry point; sibling borrow filtered by width suffix; 10s safety timer; 30s cache TTL
- `/tmp/.../global-status.json` — runtime health state (`violationCount`, GCM health)
- `docs-content/guides/status-line.md` — user-facing guide: residue troubleshooting, fast-path naming, failure-logging section, idle vs broken semantics (`⚫`), 3-stage latency chain (~90s worst-case)

## Usage
- tmux refreshes trigger `status-line-fast.cjs` on every status interval; the fast path serves from cache when fresh; a full CSL run is only triggered on cache miss or expiry
- Three sessions are monitored: `rapid-automations`, `coding`, `sketcher`; each has its own width-suffixed cache file
- The documented end-to-end latency is ~90s worst-case (cache TTL + coordinator probe interval + tmux refresh)

## Troubleshooting
- **Ghost/residue characters**: `CELL_DRIFT_BUFFER` may be insufficient — verify the +2 offset is present in `leftPadToStableCellWidth`; test outside VS Code (Terminal.app/iTerm2) to isolate xterm.js rendering
- **Trailing digit next to OKB segment**: Cell-width mismatch from ⚠️ emoji — confirm 🟡 is used everywhere for OKB warning state in both `combined-status-line.js` and `status-line-fast.cjs`
- **`⚠️ SYS:TIMEOUT` in status bar**: CSL is timing out only in certain pane contexts; check `csl-failures.jsonl`; verify the 10s safety timer in `status-line-fast.cjs` is correctly set
- **`[Status Offline]`**: Stale cache past the freshness threshold caused fallback to a synchronous CSL run that itself failed; check cache file timestamps and coordinator health
- **All coordinator badges degraded at once**: `getCoordinatorState` cascade — a transient curl failure to `:3034/health/state`; verify hardening is in place
- **Cross-width artifacts**: Sibling borrow is using a cache from a different pane width; verify borrow logic filters by `-w${paneWidth}.txt` suffix
- **`[🏥⚠️]` persists but GCM is healthy**: Check `violationCount` in `global-status.json`; if > 0, inspect recent observations for `[Raw]` tags
- **Project shows stale/wrong icon despite healthy rollup**: Heartbeat override should force 🟢 — verify the `'healthy'` rollup check is present in `combined-status-line.js`
- **`⚫` shown instead of expected status**: `isUserActive()` is returning false — session is idle; this is correct behavior distinguishing idle from broken

metadata.scope: tmux statusline rendering chain: `status-line-fast.cjs` → `combined-status-line.js`

topic: Observation Pipeline — ObservationWriter and Deduplication
summary:
## Purpose
Receives fired observations from the ETM, deduplicates them, obtains LLM summaries via the LLM CLI proxy, and persists them to SQLite for display in the system health dashboard. Also exports observations to JSON for cold-store backup and recovery, with tombstone tracking to prevent deleted records from being resurrected.

## Architecture
- **Deduplication thresholds**: intent-only Jaccard similarity threshold is **0.45** (lowered from 0.7); a stem-aware containment branch triggers at **>0.7** containment ratio with a ≥4 keyword floor to gate false positives. Both reduce false negatives (missed duplicates) while avoiding over-merging
- **Sliding window**: dedup operates over a 10-observation sliding window
- **Hash-collision dedup bug**: empty `userContent` causes every exchange in a session to hash to the same value (`6d37c4ec`), silently discarding all generated summaries without writing to the DB
- **Inode-rotation detection**: compares inodes on each write cycle and automatically reopens the database connection when the underlying SQLite file has been rotated or replaced, preventing split-brain scenarios
- **LLM summarization**: calls LLM CLI proxy; absent `llmModel`/`llmProvider` fields indicate silent summarization failure → entry stored as `[Raw]`
- **Redaction**: `ConfigurableRedactor` replaces user IDs, email addresses, OAuth tokens, and file paths using patterns from `redaction-patterns.json`
- **Tombstone resurrection prevention** (`ObservationExporter.js`): deleted/tombstoned observation IDs are tracked and explicitly excluded during export — prevents a data-integrity regression where deleted records could be silently re-imported during subsequent export→restore cycles
- **Service restart**: apply changes via `launchctl kickstart -k` targeting the `obs_api` service; poll `localhost:12436` to confirm health

## Key Files
- `src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js` — write path; dedup logic (Jaccard 0.45, containment 0.7/≥4 keywords); LLM summarization call; redaction integration; inode-rotation detection
- `src/live-logging/ObservationExporter.js` — shared export module; tombstone resurrection prevention (deleted IDs excluded from all export operations)
- `src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — two-tier aggregation built on raw observations
- `redaction-patterns.json` — configures `ConfigurableRedactor` patterns; must include OAuth token prefixes (`gho_`, `ghp_`, `github_pat_`)

## Usage
Written to by ETM processes; read by the dashboard observations API (`/api/observations`) and by `ObservationConsolidator` for daily/weekly synthesis. Restart `obs_api` after any code changes via `launchctl kickstart -k`. Export output feeds the JSON cold-store and serves as the recovery source for `SafeDatabase.js` self-healing restores.

## Troubleshooting
- **Observations stop updating / gap in dashboard**: Two likely causes: (1) hash collision on empty `userContent` — verify `userContent` is non-empty before hashing; (2) stale file handle to a rotated DB — inode-rotation detection handles this automatically after restart
- **Historical duplicate entries on dashboard**: Expected for entries written under the old 0.7 gate; new 0.45 threshold is in effect for all post-restart writes
- **New duplicates appearing after restart**: Jaccard 0.45 may be too permissive for heavily rephrased identical content; the containment branch with ≥4 keyword floor is the secondary gate
- **Raw entries in DB (no LLM summary)**: Check proxy health; verify `llmModel` field presence in recent rows; run backfill script for historical raw entries
- **Raw user IDs or paths in stored observations**: Verify `ConfigurableRedactor` is active and `redaction-patterns.json` includes OAuth token prefixes
- **Deleted observations reappearing after a restore cycle**: Tombstone resurrection prevention in `ObservationExporter.js` should block this — verify the patch is deployed and that deleted IDs are being tracked before export runs

metadata.scope: src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js, ObservationExporter.js, and the write-path deduplication and export logic

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Retrieval Relevance and Deduplication
summary:
## Purpose
Ensures that the knowledge injected into agent prompts is topically relevant, non-redundant, and favors recently-verified insights — addressing historically poor signal-to-noise ratios caused by compressed MiniLM embedding scores, broken dedup logic, and absence of freshness weighting.

## Architecture
- **Embedding similarity compression**: MiniLM cosine scores cluster in the 0.75–0.82 range, making topic discrimination unreliable by score alone; Qdrant topic pre-filtering is the primary relevance gate
- **Topic pre-filtering**: `knowledge-injection-hook.js` extracts topics from the current conversation transcript and passes them to the retrieval service to pre-filter Qdrant results before RRF fusion
- **Reranking pipeline** (in `src/retrieval/retrieval-service.js`):
  1. `_applyContextBoost` — existing context-relevance boost step
  2. `_applyFreshnessRerank` — **newly added**; applies freshness decay (recency of last insight verification) and topic relevance scoring; stale or off-topic insights rank lower regardless of raw similarity score
- **Known gap**: `verificationRatio` (per-insight correctness score) does not yet influence retrieval ranking or confidence decay; `_applyFreshnessRerank` addresses recency but not accuracy weighting
- **Near-duplicate dedup** (injection-time, via `token-budget.js`): pairwise cosine similarity at **≥ 0.93** threshold before injecting insights; MiniLM cosine floor is ~0.89–0.92, making 0.90 too aggressive
- **Digest dedup**: `scripts/dedup-digests-by-embedding.js` at **0.97** threshold (high threshold required due to shared project vocabulary causing false positives at lower values)
- **Insight dedup**: `scripts/dedup-insights-by-embedding.js` — greedy pairwise merging at **≥ 0.93** (not single-linkage chaining, which causes transitive merges of unrelated items)
- **One-time cleanup results**: insights reduced 19 → 16; digests reduced 298 → 251

## Key Files
- `src/retrieval/retrieval-service.js` — reranking pipeline; `_applyContextBoost` followed by `_applyFreshnessRerank` (freshness decay + topic relevance)
- `knowledge-injection-hook.js` — topic extraction and Qdrant pre-filter call
- `token-budget.js` — per-tier caps and near-duplicate dedup at injection time (0.93 threshold for insights)
- `scripts/dedup-insights-by-embedding.js` — on-demand insight dedup; greedy pairwise merging
- `scripts/dedup-digests-by-embedding.js` — on-demand digest dedup; 0.97 threshold

## Usage
Topic filtering and freshness reranking run automatically on every hook invocation. `token-budget.js` dedup fires at injection time on every session start. Dedup scripts are run on-demand against the live DB when duplicate accumulation is suspected (after bulk consolidation runs or large data imports).

## Troubleshooting
- **Same insight injected twice at different confidence scores**: `token-budget.js` near-duplicate dedup not active or threshold too low — verify 0.93 threshold is in effect
- **Stale insights ranking above recently-verified ones**: `_applyFreshnessRerank` not wired or verification timestamps not being written to insight records — confirm the method is called after `_applyContextBoost` in `retrieval-service.js`
- **Unrelated insights merged after running dedup script**: Script using single-linkage chaining — switch to greedy pairwise merging in `dedup-insights-by-embedding.js`
- **Threshold 0.90 merging non-duplicates**: MiniLM cosine floor is 0.89–0.92; use ≥ 0.93 for insights and 0.97 for digests
- **Noise still high after topic filtering**: Qdrant may be non-functional (falling back to keyword search) — verify Qdrant health and container status; check retrieval service logs on port 3033/3848
- **`verificationRatio` not influencing results**: Accuracy-weighted ranking is a known remaining gap; `_applyFreshnessRerank` covers recency only — `verificationRatio` weighting is a future improvement

metadata.scope: Retrieval scoring pipeline in src/retrieval/retrieval-service.js and injection-time deduplication logic

topic: System Health Dashboard — Frontend Pages and Service Architecture
summary:
## Purpose
Browser-based dashboard providing live views of raw observations, daily digests, weekly insights, token usage, and system health cards — all sourced from host-side services through the port-3033 API backend.

## Architecture
Four primary page tiers:
- **Observations page** — raw per-exchange observations; canonical reference pattern for new pages
- **Digests page** (`src/pages/digests.tsx`) — daily thematic consolidation view; sticky consolidation error banner now auto-dismisses: error state is cleared whenever a successful `/api/consolidation/status` poll response is received, proving the API is reachable
- **Insights page** (`src/pages/insights.tsx`) — weekly persistent knowledge synthesis; uses `MarkdownText` component at line 72 to render `insight.summary`
- **Token Usage page** (`src/pages/token-usage.tsx`) — Recharts visualisation including treemap; **open issue**: treemap has no `Tooltip` component (smaller boxes non-interactive); **open issue**: 8 model name spelling variants from 3 uncanonicalized callsites in `server.mjs`

**Status mapping**: `mapCheckStatus` maps sub-check status strings to display states; `'unknown'` must map to neutral "Unknown", not "Offline"; `toUiStatus` in `server.js` must recognise `'passed'` as valid.

**Redux state**: `network` field must be declared in Redux state shape and populated from API response.

**Network/proxy badges**: text-only (`[N:HOME]`/`[N:CN]`, `[P:ON]`/`[P:OFF]`) — emoji avoided due to width rendering bugs.

**Markdown header rendering (open issue)**: `##` headers in insight summaries are not rendering correctly; `MarkdownText` component (line 72 of `insights.tsx`) handling unresolved.

Port assignments:
- **3031** — constraint dashboard API (Docker)
- **3032** — system health dashboard UI
- **3033** — observations/digests/insights/health API backend (Docker)
- **3030** — constraint monitor Next.js frontend (host only)

**Redaction token rendering**: `renderWithRedactionStyling` helper in `markdown-text.tsx` wraps `<USER_ID_REDACTED>` tokens in styled `<span>` elements.

## Key Files
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/digests.tsx` — Digests tier page; error-state reset on successful consolidation status poll
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/insights.tsx` — Insights tier page; uses `MarkdownText` at line 72
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/token-usage.tsx` — Token Usage page; treemap (no `Tooltip`); model name variants issue
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/system-health-dashboard.tsx` — main dashboard; `mapCheckStatus`
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/markdown-text.tsx` — `MarkdownText`; `renderWithRedactionStyling`
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/store/` — Redux state; `network` field must be declared
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — API backend (port 3033)

## Usage
Dashboard frontend served on port 3032; backend API on port 3033. Health data proxied from host coordinator at `HEALTH_COORDINATOR_URL`. Rebuild the Next.js frontend after schema changes. Consolidation error banners on the Digests page will clear automatically once the API recovers.

## Troubleshooting
- **Consolidation error banner remains after API recovers**: Older behaviour (pre-fix) — verify `digests.tsx` clears error state on successful poll response; hard-reload if running stale build
- **`##` headers not rendering in insights view**: `MarkdownText` component header parsing unresolved — investigate `markdown-text.tsx` (no fix as of current state)
- **Treemap boxes not interactive / no tooltip**: No `Tooltip` component in treemap — fix by adding `Tooltip` to `token-usage.tsx`
- **Model names show inconsistent spellings in token usage view**: Raw API strings from 3 uncanonicalized callsites in `server.mjs` — implement `canonicalizeModelName()` at each log call site
- **Network/proxy cards show "No data"**: Trace coordinator → `server.js` `handleGetHealthStatus` → Redux reducer → component chain; verify `network` field in Redux shape
- **Services showing "Offline" when status is unknown**: `mapCheckStatus` mapping `'unknown'` to `'offline'` — fix to neutral
- **"Failed to load" despite healthy API**: Rebuild the Next.js frontend; check stale browser cache
- **Digests page shows count but empty body**: `project` column missing — re-run `migrate-add-project-column.js`

topic: OKB Viewer — Issue Triage RCA Chain and Evidence Collection
summary:
## Purpose
Displays a structured RCA chain (Incidents → Symptoms → Failure Patterns → Root Causes → Resolutions) for selected incident nodes, aggregating operational evidence links from graph neighbors.

## Architecture
- **RCA chain BFS**: Walks from an Incident node through typed edges (`HAS_SYMPTOM`, `HAS_ROOT_CAUSE`, `CAUSED`, `MITIGATED_BY`, `ADDRESSES`) to group connected entities into triage sections
- **Isolated node handling**: If an entity has 0 actual edges (common for nodes ingested from MkDocs documentation rather than RCA findings), no RCA chain can be surfaced — the apparent connection count in metadata may be non-zero but reflect stale/phantom data
- **Evidence collection**: Collects evidence by BFS hop traversal from the incident node; BFS hop distance must be kept small (≤2 hops) — 3 hops across a 900+-node graph over-broadens collection
- **Domain fuzzy matching pitfall**: Entities without a matched domain fall into a `"general"` catch-all domain; querying all `general`-domain entities can inflate evidence sources to 148+ entries — the domain sweep logic was removed as a fix; BFS neighbors and direct `sourceRefs` are the correct evidence sources
- **Ops refs propagation gap**: 109+ entities ingested from MkDocs documentation have no operational refs (Argo/RaaS/Grafana links); single-hop neighbor propagation is insufficient — a 2-hop BFS-based data-level backfill is the proposed fix

## Key Files
- `integrations/operational-knowledge-management/viewer/src/components/KnowledgeGraph/IssueTriage.tsx` — RCA chain BFS, evidence aggregation, domain sweep (removed)
- `integrations/mcp-constraint-monitor/dashboard/` — hosts the triage view UI
- `scripts/backfill-evidence-links.ts` — ops ref backfill utility

## Usage
- Activated when `activeView` is set to triage mode in the Redux `uiSlice`
- Evidence links are sorted to surface operational links (Argo, Grafana, RaaS) before documentation links
- Entities with 0 actual edges produce empty triage sections — this is correct behavior for isolated nodes

## Troubleshooting
- **Empty RCA chain for a node with listed connections**: Verify the entity actually has edges in LevelDB — apparent connection counts can be stale; use the API to confirm `edges` array length
- **Evidence sources inflated (100+)**: Domain sweep logic is pulling `general`-domain entities; ensure domain sweep is removed and evidence is sourced only from BFS neighbors and direct `sourceRefs`
- **No operational refs (Argo/RaaS/Grafana) on documentation-sourced entities**: These were never linked at ingest time; run `scripts/backfill-evidence-links.ts` or implement 2-hop BFS propagation at the data level
- **UI fix not reflected after edit**: Rebuild `viewer/dist/` with `npm run build` and hard-refresh the browser (`Cmd+Shift+R`) — the container serves from the compiled bundle

metadata.scope: The `IssueTriage.tsx` component and its BFS-based RCA chain rendering and evidence aggregation logic

topic: rapid-llm-proxy Standalone Package
summary:
## Purpose
`@rapid/llm-proxy` is a domain-agnostic LLM abstraction layer shared across the OKB and coding projects. It normalizes multiple LLM providers behind a single interface, handles corporate proxy routing, VPN-aware provider selection, exposes a local HTTP proxy bridge for sandboxed Docker consumers, and tracks token usage. `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/` is the single canonical source; `integrations/llm-cli-proxy/` is dead code slated for deletion.

## Architecture
- **Providers** (`src/providers/`): `copilot-provider.ts` (reads the OAuth refresh token from `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json` and uses it directly as Bearer — there is **no** token-exchange step; enterprise vs public routing is selected by API base URL only), `claude-code-provider.ts` (CLI-based), plus API-key providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, Gemini, GitHub Models)
- **Proxy bridge** (`proxy-bridge/server.mjs`): Local HTTP server for Docker consumers; supports **5 provider backends**: 1 CLI (Copilot) + 4 HTTP (Anthropic, Copilot direct, OpenAI, Groq); each HTTP provider is keyed on API key presence for the `available` flag; uses undici `fetch` with `ProxyAgent` dispatcher for corporate proxy tunneling
- **Network detection** (`src/network-detect.ts`): three signals in priority order — env override `LLM_NETWORK_MODE`, VPN tunnel interfaces (`utun`/`tun`/`ppp`/`wg`) with private-range routes, and DNS resolution of `LLM_VPN_PROBE_HOST`. Result gates provider initialization via `network_overrides` in YAML config; on a VPN/corporate network only Copilot and (for the premium tier) claude-code are initialized — Groq/OpenAI/Anthropic are firewall-blocked
- **Token-usage tracking** (`src/token-usage.ts`): per-hour JSON exports under `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` (default `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/`); `model_raw` column preserves raw provider model strings across export → hydrate round-trips
- **LaunchAgent** (`~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.coding.llm-cli-proxy.plist`): runs `bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` which execs `proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — `server.mjs` is the canonical entry point (no compiled `dist/server.js` exists in this repo)
- **Copilot enterprise**: Enterprise tokens must NOT go through the token exchange step — use OAuth token directly as Bearer; versioned model names like `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` unsupported; use `claude-sonnet-4.6`
- **Config** (`src/config.ts`): Reads YAML config; `network_overrides` applies per-network provider preference lists; auto-routing selects highest-priority available provider when no explicit `provider` field is supplied
- **`RAPID_HOME`** env var: Required in `.env` for toolkit integration; must be set to the correct toolkit root path

## Key Files
- `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — HTTP bridge; all 5 provider backends wired with `available` flag and health-check logic
- `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/src/providers/copilot-provider.ts` — OAuth session token exchange, caching, enterprise routing
- `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/src/providers/` — all provider implementations
- `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/src/network-detect.ts` — VPN/public network detection (interface scan + DNS probe)
- `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/src/config.ts` + `src/types.ts` — YAML config loading and `network_overrides` types
- `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/.env` — includes `RAPID_HOME` for toolkit integration
_(historical: `integrations/llm-cli-proxy/` was the predecessor in-tree implementation — it has been deleted; the canonical proxy now lives in the standalone `_work/rapid-llm-proxy` repo)_

## Usage
- OKB vendors the package as a local tarball under `vendor/`; `Dockerfile` has `COPY vendor/ vendor/` before `npm ci`
- OKB routes all LLM inference through the proxy bridge via `LLM_CLI_PROXY_URL` env var (set in `docker/docker-compose.yml`), pointing to port 12435
- Coding project's MCP server (`semantic-analysis`) consumes the package; stale `dist/` must be rebuilt after package updates
- The proxy bridge must be running on the host before Docker containers start; pass its URL to containers via `LLM_CLI_PROXY_URL`
- `/raas-job/:uuid` route must be preserved in the canonical proxy server

## Troubleshooting
- **401 from Copilot API**: OAuth token in `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json` may be expired — re-authenticate via OpenCode; do not send enterprise tokens through the `/copilot_internal/v2/token` exchange endpoint
- **Docker build fails on `@rapid/llm-proxy`**: GHE auth unavailable inside Docker; ensure `vendor/` tarball exists and `Dockerfile` has `COPY vendor/ vendor/` before `npm ci`; rebuild and repack the tarball when `dist/` is out of sync with source
- **LaunchAgent running legacy proxy**: LaunchAgent plist must point to `dist/server.js`, not `server.mjs`; version mismatch causes token-usage endpoints to return errors
- **LLM calls hang on VPN**: Non-VPN providers (Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic direct) are firewall-blocked; ensure `network_overrides` restricts VPN mode to copilot-only, and `AbortController` timeouts cancel stuck TCP sockets
- **OpenAI or Groq provider not listed as available**: Provider `available` flag is keyed on API key presence — verify the key is set in `.env` and the proxy bridge was restarted after adding it

metadata.scope: The `@rapid/llm-proxy` npm package and its proxy bridge at `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/`, providing unified LLM provider routing for OKB, coding MCP server, and Docker consumers.

topic: Enhanced Transcript Monitor (ETM)
summary:
## Purpose
Monitors active Claude session transcript files, splits message streams into complete exchanges, and fires observations to the observation pipeline at each return-to-user-prompt boundary.

## Architecture
- Reads transcript JSON files from `.specstory/history/`; tracks `lastProcessedUuid` to find unprocessed exchanges
- Flushes and fires at the **next** user-prompt boundary — observation for exchange N appears after message N+1 is sent
- Spawned and respawned by PSM; a watchdog may respawn before manual fixes take effect
- **Idempotency**: `processUserPromptSetCompletion` strips prior `ps_${promptSetId}` blocks from all tranche part-files before appending the updated block, preventing files from growing past 200 KB and spawning unintended part files
- **File-lock safety**: remove-existing-block + append logic is wrapped with `proper-lockfile` (a PSM dependency) to prevent race conditions when multiple ETM instances run concurrently during heartbeat warm-up
- **Per-exchange tranche routing**: each exchange lands in the hourly file matching its own timestamp (not the prompt set's start time); `ps_id` is the linking key across multiple hourly files for long-running prompt sets
- **`CODING_TOOLS_PATH` resolution**: must resolve from the **host** path, not the `/coding` container path — inheriting the container path prevents the redactor from initialising correctly and blocks LSL file production
- Phase 34 dead-code strip removed 7 blocks / ~200 LoC from the coding ETM (legacy online learning/trajectory monitoring code)

## Key Files
- `scripts/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` — core monitor; exchange splitting, flush trigger, per-exchange tranche routing, idempotency/lock logic, `CODING_TOOLS_PATH` host-path resolution
- `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js` — receives fired observations; deduplication and DB write
- `docker/supervisord.conf` — ETM process management; `health-verifier` block removed (was `autostart=false`, causing misleading STOPPED display)

## Usage
Runs as a background process. Multiple ETM instances can run in parallel per coding agent — concurrent access is safe via `proper-lockfile`. Must be started **after** the target transcript file exists. Requires `TMUX_PANE` env var for pane-matched LSL health status; without it, `combined-status-line.js` uses the project-level fallback. Long-running prompt sets distribute across multiple hourly tranche files, recoverable via grep on `ps_id`.

## Troubleshooting
- **LSL files not produced / redactor fails to initialise**: ETM inherited `/coding` (container path) for `CODING_TOOLS_PATH` instead of host path — fix path resolution in `enhanced-transcript-monitor.js`
- **Duplicate prompt-set blocks in tranche files**: Idempotency fix should prevent this; verify `processUserPromptSetCompletion` strips prior blocks before appending
- **Race condition duplicates with concurrent ETMs**: `proper-lockfile` wraps the critical section; if duplicates appear, verify the lock is acquired before the read-modify-write cycle
- **Observations stop after restart**: ETM started before transcript file existed — restart after confirming file exists
- **Watchdog blocks fixes**: Stop the watchdog first before editing ETM
- **`health-verifier` shows STOPPED in supervisord dashboard**: Block removed from `supervisord.conf`; if still present, remove it

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Embedding Pipeline
summary:
## Purpose
Provides semantic vector search over the project's accumulated observations, digests, insights, and KG entities to enable context injection into agent prompts. Backs the standalone retrieval HTTP service (port 3848) that serves all agent adapter layers.

## Architecture
- **Embedding service** (`src/embedding-service.ts`) uses `fastembed` for local, platform-native text embedding
- **fastembed cache**: pre-warmed during the Docker **builder stage** and baked into the production image (~90MB); the `local_cache` directory is **not** bind-mounted at runtime, so cache must exist in the image or the process fails with `FATAL` at startup
- **Qdrant** stores vectors in 4 collections: `observations`, `digests`, `insights`, `kg_entities`
- **SHA-256 content hashing** (`src/content-hash.ts`) provides idempotency — items already embedded are skipped on re-run
- **Write-time hook**: a Redis pub/sub listener (`embedding-listener` supervisord process) auto-embeds new observations as they are written
- **Proxy env vars**: `supervisord.conf` is configured to **inherit** proxy environment variables (with `NO_PROXY` covering internal services) rather than clearing them, enabling external fallback fetches
- **Backfill CLI**: ingests existing data; populated 1,464 items on first run

## Key Files
- `src/embedding-service.ts` — core embedding logic, fastembed integration
- `src/content-hash.ts` — SHA-256 idempotency hashing
- `src/qdrant-collections.ts` — Qdrant collection schema definitions
- `embedding-config.json` — embedding model and collection configuration (path must be relative to compiled output location)
- `docker/Dockerfile.coding-services` — builder stage pre-warms fastembed cache; copies to production image
- `docker/supervisord.conf` — starts `embedding-listener`; inherits proxy env vars

## Usage
The backfill CLI is run once (or after bulk data imports). The `embedding-listener` Redis pub/sub daemon runs as a background supervisord process, embedding new observations automatically. The retrieval service on port 3848 is called by agent hook adapters.

## Troubleshooting
- **`embedding-listener` shows `FATAL` / "Exited too <USER_ID_REDACTED>"**: fastembed local cache is missing from the image — rebuild `Dockerfile.coding-services` so the builder stage pre-warms and copies the cache (~90MB)
- **External fetch fails from inside container**: `supervisord` was clearing proxy env vars — verify `supervisord.conf` inherits `http_proxy`/`https_proxy` and sets `NO_PROXY` for internal hosts
- **`embedding-config.json` not found at runtime**: `__dirname` in compiled output resolves to `dist/embedding/` — use `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../embedding-config.json')` or an env var absolute path
- **New observations not appearing in Qdrant**: Verify `embedding-listener` is in `RUNNING` state via supervisorctl; check Redis pub/sub event publication; manually trigger backfill CLI for missed items
- **fastembed fails to load tokenizer binaries**: `--ignore-scripts` npm install flag blocks postinstall scripts — use a separate install step without that flag for fastembed

metadata.scope: The fastembed-backed embedding service and its Docker deployment inside `coding-services`

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Hook and Agent Adapters
summary:
## Purpose
Injects relevant observations, digests, insights, working memory, and KG entities into agent prompts at session start so agents have project-specific context without manual lookup. Designed to be cross-project, cross-agent, and context-aware.

## Architecture
- **Entry point**: `knowledge-injection-hook.js` — registered in `~/.claude/settings.json` (global) for cross-project Claude coverage; fires at each session start via Claude Code's hook mechanism
- **Topic extraction**: hook extracts topics from the conversation transcript and passes them to the retrieval service for Qdrant-filtered search; skips `system-reminder` blocks to avoid transcript feedback loops
- **Retrieval client**: `pre-prompt-hook-wrapper.js` (or shared retrieval client) calls the retrieval service on port 3033 / 3848
- **Input gating**: trivial inputs filtered by `MIN_WORDS = 4` (not a token threshold); piped stdin is detected with a truthy check on `process.stdin.isTTY` (not `=== false`, which never matches when stdin is `undefined`)
- **Agent adapters**: session-start adapters exist for OpenCode, Copilot, and Mastra, wired into the launch system; Claude uses the global hook
- **Per-agent scoring**: `config/agent-profiles.json` holds tier weight multipliers per agent; each adapter passes an `agent` field to enable profile-specific RRF fusion
- **Cross-agent continuity**: `.coding/session-state.json` stores session state; injected as "Previous Session" context within a 2-hour window via `scripts/write-session-state.js`
- **Working memory**: `src/retrieval/working-memory.js` prepends a 300-token working memory prefix (live KG query for the Coding project's 8 Components) before the 700-token retrieval results; uses `?types=` (plural, CSV) parameter for the VKB API

## Key Files
- `knowledge-injection-hook.js` — Claude hook entry point; topic extraction; transcript feedback loop prevention
- `pre-prompt-hook-wrapper.js` — stdin gating, isTTY check, MIN_WORDS filter
- `src/retrieval/working-memory.js` — KG working memory prefix; filters out Project/System node leakage from component <USER_ID_REDACTED>
- `config/agent-profiles.json` — per-agent RRF fusion weight multipliers
- `scripts/write-session-state.js` — writes session state for cross-agent continuity
- `~/.claude/settings.json` — global hook registration for cross-project Claude support

## Usage
Fires automatically at each Claude Code session start. OpenCode/Copilot/Mastra adapters are triggered via their respective launch scripts. The hook injects up to ~12 insights, 2 digests, system health, `CLAUDE.md`, global rules, `MEMORY.md`, and working memory into the system prompt.

## Troubleshooting
- **Hook fires in `coding` project but not others**: Hook was previously registered only in the project-local settings; ensure it is in `~/.claude/settings.json` (global)
- **No injection / silent skip**: Check `process.stdin.isTTY` — must use truthy check, not `=== false`; verify prompt has ≥ 4 words
- **Irrelevant content injected (wrong project)**: Qdrant topic pre-filtering may be failing; verify retrieval service is running and Qdrant is healthy; check that topics are being extracted from the transcript correctly
- **Injected content loops back as new context**: `system-reminder` blocks in the transcript were being re-read as user context; ensure the hook skips `system-reminder` blocks during topic extraction
- **Working memory shows wrong project or duplicates**: Verify `?types=Component` (plural) is used in the VKB API call; client-side filter should exclude Project/System nodes from component result lists

metadata.scope: The hook scripts and agent adapters that retrieve and inject knowledge context into agent prompts at session start.

topic: Observations API Server — Host-API Pattern
summary:
## Purpose
A standalone host-side HTTP API server that wraps `observations.db`, replacing direct SQLite access from dashboard containers with HTTP forwarding. Follows the established host-API wrapper pattern used for OKB/VKB and LevelDB integrations. Also embeds the consolidation runner and Qdrant-based semantic retrieval to avoid multi-process race conditions.

## Architecture
- **Pattern**: Host-API wrapper — the server runs on the host, holds the only live connection to `observations.db`, and exposes REST endpoints; dashboard handlers inside Docker forward requests via HTTP
- **Endpoints**: Observation projects, digests, insights, write endpoints for new observations, and `/api/consolidation/status` (includes freshness timestamp data so the coordinator can poll observation state without opening SQLite directly, avoiding read-write conflicts)
- **Stuck-worker detection**: `_bumpHeartbeat` refreshes `_lastStderrAt` when real stderr has been silent for >30 seconds, using the heartbeat timer as proof the event loop is alive. This prevents false-positive "worker stuck" banners during long async LLM calls that produce no stderr output
- **Stuck threshold vs. keepalive**: The threshold is checked against `_lastStderrAt`; `_bumpHeartbeat` proactively resets it so `stderrAgeMs` stays under 30s during long LLM calls
- **ObservationWriter integration**: The server integrates `ObservationWriter` directly; a warm-on-startup path initializes the writer at launch
- **Host-side consolidation**: Consolidation runner logic is embedded directly in the API server (not a separate spawned process) to avoid race conditions
- **Retrieval/embedding**: Qdrant and embedding connectivity are integrated for semantic retrieval of observations
- **docker-compose registration**: Declared in `docker/docker-compose.yml`; dashboard handler blocks previously accessing `observations.db` directly have been replaced with HTTP forwarders
- **Shutdown handler**: Cleanly closes DB connections and stops the consolidation runner

## Key Files
- `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` — complete host-side API server; all DB access, consolidation, retrieval logic, freshness timestamps on `/api/consolidation/status`, `_bumpHeartbeat` stuck-worker keepalive
- `docker/docker-compose.yml` — registers the observations API service and wires dashboard HTTP forwarding

## Usage
- Start the server on the host before launching Docker services so the dashboard can reach it
- Dashboard containers must not open `observations.db` directly — all access routes through HTTP to this server
- The coordinator should poll `/api/consolidation/status` over HTTP (not open the DB file) to read observation state; the endpoint returns freshness timestamps alongside status data
- Duplicate `transcript-monitor` processes should be terminated before starting the server to avoid write race conditions

## Troubleshooting
- **False-positive "worker stuck" banner**: `_bumpHeartbeat` must refresh `_lastStderrAt` when stderr has been silent >30s — verify this keepalive logic is present in `observations-api-server.mjs`; the fix was that `_lastStderrAt` was previously only refreshed on actual stderr output, not by heartbeat
- **Dashboard shows stale/missing observations**: Verify `observations-api-server.mjs` is running on the host and reachable from the container at the configured port
- **Write conflicts**: Ensure no other `ObservationWriter` or `transcript-monitor` instances are running alongside the API server — terminate duplicates first
- **Coordinator opening DB directly**: Coordinator should use the HTTP endpoint `/api/consolidation/status` instead; direct SQLite opens cause read-write conflicts
- **obs_api crash/recovery (new PID with ~1min uptime)**: SIGTERM events visible in the observations-api log; check `start-services-robust.js` EADDRINUSE retry logic and `process.ppid` in SIGTERM log lines for tracing unexpected termination sources
- **Qdrant sync gap**: After bulk fixes to observations/digests/insights, re-run the backfill path in the API server to re-index updated records into Qdrant

metadata.scope: The host-side HTTP API server (`scripts/observations-api-server.mjs`) wrapping `observations.db`, including consolidation runner, stuck-worker detection, and embedding/retrieval.
